transcript: Detroit, how’re ya doin’ toniiight?! [delay & pitch]
[sample of Wayne Bent cultists cheering, shouting, “Liberty!”]
Hi! Thank you. Thank you for being here. Thank you for havin’ me tonight. Thank you very much.
[delay] Woo! Love to have a little fun tonight here and, uh, make some calls. If you want to have a little chit-chat, that’d be sweet.
We—we got a—I took some numbers from the crowd.
I got another number dialin’ right now. Uh, just for a sec, let’s see if they answer.
[sample of Wayne Bent ] Yes. Yes. Well, um.

Oh, hi! Yeah, this is Bobby. Yeah, I’m your new neighbor over here. I just moved in. Uh, thanks for, uh, taking my call. I wasn’t able to get ‘hold o’ ya earlier in the week. But, uh, I’m goin’ outta town. Gotta go outta town for about two weeks, so I just went ahead and had my mail forwarded to you while I’m outta town just for two weeks. I just wanted ta let ya know—
Oh, yeah! I’m your new neighbor. I don’t really know anyone else in the area—
Just to your house, there. Um, I just wanted to let ya know so you weren’t surprised when it started to show up, okay?
Okay, so which—which house? I mean, I’m in the midst of a lot of moves, so I don’t know which one you’re talking about.
Oh, yeah! Boy, I’m—I’m real new to the area!
[clicks tongue] Freddie, up at the filling station, gave me your number. Um, it’s just for two weeks. So, if you could have that sorted for me?—
—You know what, you could—y—that’s okay. But thank you for calling.
Oh, lady! She’s gone! She’s gone. People—Detroit [delay] Woo! [pitch & speed] Hi!
I’m going right ahead into another number. Here we go.
[sample of Wayne Bent ] Um. I stood and suddenly—
Hi. This is Devin. What’s goin’ on? I’m your, uh, new neighbor. I just kinda moved in, here.

Ah, my name’s Devin, here. I just moved in. And, ah, just got your phone number from my buddy, uh, at the filling station ‘round the bend, there. I just wanted to tall ya real quick. I noticed you were outside a bunch this week. Um. Tomorrow, would you mind stayin’ inside? I—I’m a whipmaster. I just wanna practice usin’ my whip outside. Just wanna make sure the area’s clear ’n safe and everything. Would that be alright with ya?

Oh, I just live, uh, just adjacent to you. You know, I got a 28-foot horsehwip. [exhales] Get that thing spinnin’ around. I just wanted to be safe. Don’t want any liability, responsibility—any o’ that good stuff.
—So, would you stay inside or is that, uh, is that alright with ya or…? [delay]
No, I have—I have plans tomorrow. So I’m definitely gonna be outside. Yeah—
—Um, no, I—just—just this once, could ya not do it and stay indoors? [sniffs] I get this thing spinnin’ around, it’s like a propeller flyin’ through the air! [exhales]
How did you get this number? I don’t actually even live where you live. I can hear people laughing in the background. But…
No. I thought that was on your end—
—I’m just curious how you even got my number.
From Old Ironsides up at the filling station; guy’s a friend o’ mine up ‘ere. [exhales] He used to wo—
—I don’t even know what that is. I don’t—I live in Michigan.
I do, too! That’s what I’m sayin’. He—he works at Reuters. I mean, guy knows everybody. Knows everybody up ‘ere!
—So I’ve got another number already ringing. And, ah. Just gimme five seconds, here.
[“Man(1)” of Clove And Hoof restaurant enters call]
—I need some, uh—what the—what the fuck? You did not just hang up on me. You did not just do that! Okay! Well, let’s just get him back on the line. Here we go. That’s fine!
[sample of Wayne Bent ] I swear it!
[sample of Wayne Bent ] It is—it is a defining moment and—
[sample of Wayne Bent ] It is a defining moment—
—[sample of Wayne Bent disciple] This means literally—
Yeah, why’d you hang up, first off?
—I’m a customer here, guy. Don’t hang up on me in the future, alright? Ya got me?
I—I’m—I’m really busy. You don’t know where you called, so how can I help you?
I need to place a purchase, big guy, and I don’t like your trip that you’re on. So, uh, you wanna get serious now?—
—I can’t hear ya. Can you say that again?—
—I need to place an order, dickhead! Alright? You hear me now or…?
—you’re breaking up on me, g—I can’t hear ya—
—I’m gonna break your teeth. Plain and simple! You got me? [exhales]
No, I can’t hear you. You’re breaking up.
I’m gonna pull your lips and put ‘em around your nostrils. You hear that or…? [blows through lips]
—I—I—I’m sorry, are you—are you threatening me on the phone?
Uh, no, it’s a guarantee, more or less, ‘cause I’m gonna pull your lips and spread ‘em out, put your lips—
—Okay. Is there anything I can help you with tonight or no?
Did not like that guy! Did not care for him!
Woo! [delay & pitch-up] So, you’ll have that from time to time, as well. [sniffs]
This is another place. It is ringing. And, uh, let’s see what happens with that.
Hey, how ya doin’ tonight up ‘ere? [exhales]
Good, yeah! Can you guys, uh, go ‘head and close down early tonight?
Can we “go ‘head and close down early tonight”!
Yeah! Yeah. ‘Cause I wanna, uh, use the parking lot, practice my horse whip. Got about a 28-foot horse whip I wanna get workin’ on. I’m a whipmaster—
—Oh, you know what? Oh! That’s—that’s a great excuse! Lemme get right on that one!
Okay. So just shut everything down and just quiet down and, uh, just kinda button your lip a little bit, alright?
Good deal, guy! And, uh, rub my feet when I’m done; that’s how I like to work, that’s how I like to operate—
—Oh! Do I start with the left or the right?
Uh-huh! Oh, get—get goin’ and I’ll, uh, I’ll coach ya!
—what I like, what I don’t like and, uh—
—my likes, dislikes, things like that, alright?
And I’m gonna treat ya to a little, uh, slab o’ meat, when we’re done. Sound good or…?
Oh, no! I—I don’t go that way, personally, but I d—you know, I don’t—I don’t—I don’t, uh, I don’t judge, so…
I mean actual muscle cuts from a calf! I wasn’t talking—
—Like the calf of your leg or like a cow calf?
No, a cow calf, not my anatomy, sir—
—But if you wanna talk musculature, we could be here all night! You know, I’m ripped, guy!
[ samples of Wayne Bent ] Yes. Yes— Well, um—
[voicemail (“VM”) greeting of Del Campo International Foods’ restaurant plays]
[ samples of Wayne Bent ] It wasn’t— Yes—
We are located at 55 Webster Street in Jack London Sauare—
[ samples of Wayne Bent ] And nakedness is another symbol of our relationship with God—
—Our restaurant hours of operation are Monday thru Thursday from 11:00 AM to 10:00 PM…
…and to 11:00 PM with brunch service from 10:00 until we’re closed—
—Okay! Hell of a deal! Hell. Of. A. Deal! Thank you for your time up there, and, uh—
Oh, yeah? I’ll be the judge of that—
I’ll be the judge, guy! Ya feel me?—
—Right on. I feel you. Is there anything else I can help you with, sir?
[reverb & delay] Uh, no! That’ll do it. [exhales]
Alright. I wanted—Well I wanna thank you for giving me a good laugh this evening.
Okay. So, um, hi! Detroit! [reverb] Woo! [delay & pitch-down]
Thank you again for havin’ me.
Glass Nickel. Interesting. Interesting. W—what’s those, uh, digits?
“0880”. Let’s make this a good one. Here we go! Glass Nickel Pizza. Okay. Here we go, here we go, a-here we frickin’ go! [sfx] [delay & reverb] Okay, here we go—
[“Man(3)” of Glass Nickel Pizza Co. enters call]
—Glass Nickel. How can I help you?
[delay & reverb] Hi! How’re you doin’ tonight? Hey!—
Good! I’d like to place an order, please. [to self] Oh, fu—
—I’m just gonna come pick it up, if I can pick up, please?—
Okay, what can I get for you?

Um, can I get, like, a small pineapple [delay & reverb] and, uh, and, like, a extra large pineapple? And, ah, just go ahead with that and then go do, like, a triple, uh, triple pepperoni—just a regular-sized pizza but with a triple portion—
—Uh, I’m sorry, I—I can’t really hear ya.
—So, “small pineapple, large pineapple”, is what I got.

Yeah, and like a triple portion of pepperoni, you know what I mean? Just whatever the normal portion is, just times that by, uh, [delay & reverb] by triple and then just get that goin’ on that one for me. And then maybe some breadsticks—
—You want—you want triple pine—[chuckles]—you want triple pineapple—you want triple pepperoni on the large pineapple?
[delay & reverb] I want triple pineapple on the triple pepperoni and then if you could put pepper—
—Yeah, there’s a delay on the phone—
—so I—I can’t really hear anything you’re, uh—
—If you could put pep—pineapple on the breadsticks and then just trip—
—[delay & reverb] triple that amount—whatever you would normally put, just triple that and then we could put that together with my pizza and then just put that together.
Uh, okay. I’m havin’ a hard time hearin’ ya, so right now, I got a small pineapple pizza, a large pineapple pizza—
—Small pineapple pizza. Okay. You got a small pineapple pizza, uh-huh.
Uh-huh. And a large pineapple pizza.
Oh! And then the breadsticks. [delay & reverb] Now, if you could take the breadsticks—
—[delay & reverb] If you—you have—you have breadsticks, right?
[delay & reverb] Yeah, if you could take the pineapples and triples what the normal portion you do—put ‘em on the breadsticks—so put it together that way, that would be the best for my order—
—I’m—I’m sorry. I can’t—I can’t hear ya.
Yeah. I guess it’s just on my end, then.
[samples of Wayne Bent ] Yes. Yes— Well, um— Yes—
Okay, so then what—what do you need me to repeat?
Um, I—so an order of breadsticks?
Yes. Just a regular order of breadsticks?
[delay & reverb] Well y—regular breadsticks, yeah, but if you took the pineapple—the, like, that you put on the pizza—?

—[delay & reverb] Is there any way you could triple, like, multiply the normal portion that you would use and put it on the breadsticks? That would be the best for—to put that with my pizza. That’d be good.
It’s weird! Every time you s—so when [laughs]
Every time I say something you repeat that you can’t hear—you’re like repeating yourself, or something. Can’t really understand what you’re saying—
—Yeah. Maybe there is a delay on both of our phones that—on both sides. Um—
—Can’t really understand what you’re sayin’ but can you do that with the breadstick?
I—I really couldn’t hear anything that you said with the breadsticks—
—[delay & reverb] Like, the same pineapple you put on the pizza, you know?
—[delay & reverb] Just put that on the pi—on the breadstick and then put it in triple the quantity that you would put on the pizza but just put it on—on the breadsticks instead of that.
We can’t put pineapples on the breadsticks.
[delay & reverb] Well, I mean, okay, you can put pepperoni on the breadsticks instead and kinda go it that way instead? You know?

Just for this once. I know, maybe it’s a custom order, I don’t know. I have—I don’t have a copy of your menu. [inhales] [delay & reverb] But if you just took the breadsticks and triple the pineapple on the br—
—We could do pineapple on cheese bread, if you’d like—

—[delay & reverb] Yeah! If you put the cheese bread on the pineapple, bud, on the pepperoni, that’d be super—and put it on the breadstick, that’d be good—instead of the pineapple, you know? If you—I—I know you’re particular about the way you like to—it’s what I’m hearing—you’re particular about the way you do that. So—

—don’t do the breadsticks. Scratch the breadstick. [delay & reverb] Take the cheese stick and put the triple, ah, pineapple instead of the pepperoni, on the cheese stick and the pepperoni—on the pepper—

—large order [delay & reverb] or something like in the pepperoni? Oh, and on the pepp—and I’m—I’m being told I wanna add, uh, sausage onto the pepperoni pizza. So triple sausage, triple pepperoni as well as the pineapple and something like that. [delay] Can we do all three of those? And, um, looking for a coupon, here. I think I have a coupon somewhere—
—I’ve got—so triple—[laughs]—triple pineapple on the cheese bread with pi—?
—[delay & reverb] Triple pineapple and something on the cheese bread, uh-huh—
—[delay & reverb] With pepperoni on it, yeah. Yeah, exactly! Okay, so you’re gonna do that. I’m with you, uh-huh.
—You’re—you’re like dropping out. I can’t really hear what you’re saying. But go ‘head. [delay & reverb] So ye—so r—
—and triple pepperoni on the order of cheese bread, is that right?—

—[delay & reverb] I wanna trip—yeah, exactly. Exactly, right, yeah! [delay] So, if you could put those together—put the pineapple on the cheese bread and then put the pineapple and the pepperoni on the pizza and I was being told I wanna add sausage on the pep—on the regular pizza if you could put those together and then you could do—and then you could put those together for me that’d be excellent! Super. Super, super. Super! And I have a coup—
—It sounds like there’s, like, a million of you talkin’ at once [chuckles]
[samples of Wayne Bent ] Yes— No!— No!— I swear it!—
[delay & reverb] And then I’ve got—I’ve got a coupon someplace. I’m not sure. Not sure where that is, but, uh, I’m looking f—
—Okay, I—I’m sorry, man, we’re gettin’ busy. I—if you wanna call back from another phone, I can complete your order.
[delay & reverb] Well, no. I’ve got the—I’ve got the coupon right here!
Okay, yeah, I can’t—I don’t have your order completed. I can’t understand what your—what you were ordering, so—
—Oh, which part do you need me to repeat?
It’d be best if you just called back from another, uh, phone number, I think.
Well four—uh, my phone number is, uh, 313 [delay] -5180 [delay]—
—I’ve got caller ID here, so I got the—I got your phone number, here—

—3-88-35 [delay & reverb]—oh, you got—good, okay, great. So, if you just put it all together I’ll pay cash when I get there. I’ll be there in—what?—45 minutes to an hour—something like th—[delay] ‘Bout fourty-five minutes to an hour? Is that enough time or…?
Uh, carry out time is different—I haven’t been able to complete your order ‘cause I can’t really hear what you’re—
—Okay, what part do you need me to repeat?
I got a large pineapple pizza and a 10-inch pineapple pizza—is that someone laughin’?
I thought that was on your end.
Uh, no! Nobody here but me. [sniffs]
Okay. Yeah, I’m just gonna have to have you—
—Oh, and the cheese bread! The cheese bread!—
—call from another phone number, man. I can’t complete the order.
The cheese bread was the only thing I need left.
A regular order of cheese bread?

[delay & reverb] No. I need to put the pepperoni and the pineapple on the cheese bread and b—and do triple the portion. So just take the pineapple and the pepperoni, put it on the cheese bread, put it all together, and then just put it o—like pineapple cheese bread with pepperoni on it, too. Just something like— [delay] And then we’ll be good!
[delay & reverb] Okay? Good. Good deal, good deal. Okay, thank you! [laughs] [inhales] Detroit, thank you so much! You guys rule! Detroit!
[delay & reverb] Good night! Thank you so much! You are the best! Woo! [delay]
[continual sample of Wayne Bent cultists cheering, shouting, “Liberty!”]]
[delay & reverb] Thank you! Thank you! Thank you! Thank you so much! Thank you so much! Thank you! Goodnight! Detroit, thank you! Thank you! Thank you so much! Detroit, woo, Detroit!
Goodnight! You’re the best! I love you! Detroit! Woo, Detroit! Thank you guys! I love you! You’re the best! I love you! Goodnight!
